Aims                                                                                                                                Revision of Linear Algebra



   1. Diagonalise a matrix (when possible) by using its                                                                               Warning
      eigenvalues and eigenvectors.                                                                                                     I We start with a quick review of key topics from first year
   2. Diagonalise a real symmetric matrix using its eigenvalues                                                                           linear algebra: matrices, inverses, determinants, systems
      and orthonormal eigenvectors.                                                                                                       of linear equations, Gaussian Elimination and row
                                                                                                                                          reduction.
   3. Apply matrix diagonalisation to the study of quadratic
                                                                                                                                        I If you are not confident with this material you should look
      surfaces and systems of ODEs.

                                                                                                                         Transpose of a matrix

                                                                                                                          Definition 4
                                                                                                                            I We say that an n × m matrix B = [bij ] is the transpose
                                                                                                                              of an m × n matrix A = [aij ] if

                                                                                                                                       bij = aji   for 1 ≤ i ≤ n and 1 ≤ j ≤ m.

                                                                                                                               In this case, we put A⊤ = B.
                                                                                                                            I The ith row of A⊤ has the same entries as the ith
                                                                                                                              column of A.
                                                                                                                            I The ith column of A⊤ has the same entries as the ith
                                                                                                                              row of A.
                                                                                                                            I A matrix A is said to be symmetric if A = A⊤ .

det(A) = −2                                 .   .   .     . . . .     . . . .     . . . .       .   .       .   .   .                                                   .   .   .     . . . .     . . . .     . . . .        .    .       .   .   .

                                                                                                                             Eigenvalues and Eigenvectors – Diagonalisation
                                                                                                                               Definition 8
                                                                                                                               Given a square matrix A, a non-zero vector ⃗v is said to be an
                                                                                                                               eigenvector of A if

                                                                                                                                                 A⃗v = λ⃗v   for some λ ∈ C.                                                       (4)

                                                                                                                               The number λ is called the eigenvalue of A associated with ⃗v .
                                                                                                                               Notes

  If A is an invertible matrix with eigenvector ⃗v and                                                                            The eigenvectors associated with distinct eigenvalues of a
